editorial_positioning: A warm, polished home publication that translates good design into real-room decisions for everyday readers.

should_feel_like: a calm stylist with good taste
a useful editorial guide rather than a sales page

headline_style.description: Headlines should be clear, tasteful, and specific. They should promise a recognizable decorating point of view, not just a search phrase.
prefer: real constraints like small rooms, layout, light, budget, or material choices

intro_style.description: Open with a quick, specific editorial read on why the topic matters in a real home, then turn toward what the reader will understand or be able to do.
must_do: anchor the intro in the actual room, material, mood, or design problem
sound like this article could only introduce this topic
move from point of view into practical direction quickly
avoid: opening with generic trend commentary alone
long scene-setting that delays the advice

conclusion_style.description: Conclusions should feel lightly edited and decisive. End with a calm takeaway, first step, or framing thought rather than a generic recap.
prefer: encouraging clarity about what to try first
a sense of editorial edit rather than summary filler

principles: Good rooms feel intentional before they feel expensive.
Texture, proportion, and light usually matter more than adding more decor.
A home should feel lived-in and composed, not over-styled.
Readers should leave with a better eye, not just a shopping list.

variation_rules.description: Keep the voice consistent without sounding canned.
rules: Do not repeat signature phrases multiple times in one article.
Use the editorial point of view as a lens, not a slogan.
Let persona, angle, and topic change the rhythm while the publication voice stays recognizable.
